name: gRPC Server Reflection exposes internal services in production
description:
Probes the gRPC Server Reflection endpoint over HTTP/1.1 (so the scan can hit it without an HTTP/2 client). Vulnerable when the reflection service is mapped — it leaks the service catalogue + method signatures to anyone who can reach the port.

Two detection paths, because a reflection-enabled server answers differently depending on whether gRPC-Web is also enabled:

1. **Plain gRPC** (the common shape: `MapGrpcReflectionService()` with no gRPC-Web middleware). An HTTP/1.1 request to a *mapped* gRPC route is rejected by the gRPC handler with `415` and a `Grpc-Status` trailer-as-header, and an EMPTY body; an unmapped path returns a plain `404` with no `Grpc-*` header. So `415` + the presence of `Grpc-Status` proves the reflection route exists. This is the header-based branch — the body carries no evidence here, which is exactly why a body-only predicate produced a false negative.
2. **gRPC-Web enabled**. The request is served and the body carries the service list — matched by the JSON/regex/substring branches.

vulnerability.remediation: In ASP.NET Core, drop `app.MapGrpcReflectionService()` from the production build, OR gate it behind `.RequireAuthorization("Admin")`. Verify with `grpcurl -plaintext <host> list` — should return "Failed to list services: ...". Some incident-response runbooks ALWAYS treat reflection in prod as a finding regardless of authentication, since it widens the attack surface even for valid admin users.

steps._protocolNote: DO NOT 'correct' protocol to 'grpc' YET — it would silently un-fire this template against the released CLI. Bowire's scanner only replays HTTP-class transports (ScanCommand.IsHttpClassProtocol); GRPC was missing from that list, so a 'grpc' value made the scanner skip the probe entirely. This probe is a plain HTTP/1.1 POST, so it rides an HTTP-class value instead. Bowire main has since added GRPC/GRPC-WEB (Kuestenlogik/Bowire@4ffe579b — verified: the honest value then fires), but validate.yml installs the PUBLISHED CLI. Flip this to 'grpc' and delete this note only once a release carrying that commit is out, otherwise the gate starts skipping this template.
